reasoning: Discord is actively preparing for a 2025 IPO, having hired investment bankers (Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan) in March 2025, and recently appointed a new CEO, Humam Sakhnini (former Activision Blizzard vice chairman), who assumed the role on April 28, 2025 specifically to lead the IPO process. A lot of the groundwork has been laid. However, there are only about 5.5 weeks between the May 23, 2025 knowledge cutoff and the July 1, 2025 resolution date. With a brand-new CEO just getting situated and reports of recent market volatility in May 2025 delaying other IPOs, Discord might opt to wait until the third quarter (incorporating Q2 financial results after June 30) to formally file its S-1 or announce its plans, rather than rushing a June filing using Q1 numbers. While a June filing remains very plausible, the tight deadline and recent executive transition constrain the probability of it happening before July 1.
